examples.question: Perfect, thank you. For the outlets, we are coming from Canada which are 120v compared to the 220v in Korea. For certain chargers, is there adapters for type A and type B prongs to type F and C?
examples.answer:
Hi there 😊✨
About 110V outlet converters / voltage converters — here’s the detailed guide:
• Universal power strip in every bedroom 🧩🔌
Each room is equipped with a universal multi-outlet power strip, so most travelers can plug in their chargers easily.
• Do you need a “down converter”? ⚡️
In most cases, no. For modern electronics like:
phone chargers
laptop chargers
camera / power bank chargers
tablet chargers
these are usually dual-voltage (100–240V), so they work perfectly in Korea with the right plug fit.
✅ Tip: Please check your adapter label — if it says “Input: 100–240V”, you do NOT need a converter.
• Important: We do NOT provide a down converter
We don’t keep voltage converters at the home — because they’re rarely needed for most guests’ devices.
• When a converter might be necessary (rare)
If you’re bringing high-power or motor/heating appliances such as:
hair dryer / curler / straightener (some models)
electric shaver (older models)
travel iron
CPAP that is NOT dual-voltage
then a converter may be needed only if the device is 110V-only.
If you think you may need one, just tell us the device name + a quick photo of the power label (where voltage is written), and we’ll guide you exactly what to prepare 🙏✨

examples.question: Such as: The insurance suppose to cover all people? From the period of?
examples.answer:
Thank you very much for choosing COZE for your stay in Korea.
Your reservation already includes a complimentary Basic (Orange / Normal grade) domestic travel insurance plan.
 During your stay in Korea, the plan provides coverage within the policy limits in the following situations:
✅ Basic – Orange Plan (included for free)
Accidental death or permanent disability: up to KRW 50,000,000
Medical expenses in Korea for injuries from accidents (insured & non-insured treatment): up to KRW 10,000,000 each
Personal liability if you accidentally injure someone or damage property: up to KRW 10,000,000
Not covered: treatment for illness (e.g. colds, fever), death due to disease, loss/damage of baggage, and special non-reimbursed treatments such as manual therapy, injections, MRI/MRA, etc.
To activate the insurance, please send us the following details for all guests before check-in:
Full name as in passport
Date of birth
Nationality
Passport number
Once we receive this information, we will register the policy to match your stay dates.
If you would like broader protection including illness-related medical expenses, you can upgrade to the following paid plans:
✨ Standard – Green Plan (KRW 5,000 per person per day)
Accidental death or permanent disability: up to KRW 100,000,000
Medical expenses in Korea for injuries (insured & non-insured): up to KRW 30,000,000 each
Personal liability: up to KRW 30,000,000
Loss or damage of personal belongings (excluding loss): up to KRW 100,000
Not covered: death from disease, illness-related treatment, and special non-reimbursed therapies (manual therapy, injections, MRI/MRA, etc.)
✨ High-end – Red Plan (KRW 10,000 per person per day)
Accidental death or permanent disability: up to KRW 100,000,000
Death or 80%+ severe disability due to disease: up to KRW 20,000,000
Medical expenses in Korea for both injuries and illnesses (insured & non-insured): up to KRW 30,000,000 each
Personal liability: up to KRW 30,000,000
Loss or damage of personal belongings (excluding loss): up to KRW 150,000
Non-reimbursed therapies such as manual therapy, extracorporeal shock-wave therapy, and prolotherapy: up to KRW 3,500,000
Non-reimbursed injections: up to KRW 2,500,000; MRI/MRA scans: up to KRW 3,000,000
Any additional premium for upgraded plans will simply be added to your final bill at check-out.
 If you’d like to upgrade or have any questions, please feel free to let me know anytime 😊
Warmly,
 Gaya, Guest Care Team of COZE Hospitality 3.0

examples.answer:
Hey there, shopaholics!
Get ready for some lightning-fast shopping with Coupang—Korea’s one-stop online marketplace for everything from groceries to gadgets. Here’s a quick look at how to make the most of Coupang’s Rocket Delivery (로켓배송) and our convenient concierge service!
🚀 Rocket Delivery (로켓배송) 101
Coupang’s signature Rocket Delivery comes in several colors, each offering a different shipping speed or service type:
💙 Light Blue Rocket: Delivered within 24 hours
🏙 Ultramarine Rocket: Early Morning (Dawn) Delivery, arriving before sunrise
💜 Purple Rocket: Overseas Direct Import, usually taking about 1 week
💚 Green Rocket: Grocery Delivery, often arriving on the same day or by next-day dawn for fresh produce
🛒 How to Shop with Our Concierge
Search for Products: Open the Coupang app or website and enter your desired product name or category.
Filter by “로켓배송”: Pick items marked with a Rocket icon (💙, 🏙, 💜, or 💚) for speedy or specialized delivery.
Share the Item: Instead of adding to your cart, simply tap the “Share” button for each product you want and send it to our Concierge channel.
We’ll Handle the Rest: Once we have your shared links, we’ll complete the purchase on your behalf—no Korean payment method or account needed!
💰 Payment Options & Additional Fees
Cash: You can settle your bill with us in any major currency, or even pay with USDT or other cryptocurrencies. This is generally the most cost-effective option.
Local Bank Transfer: We can accept transfers in your currency via a local bank account, though please note there’s a 10% VAT added.
Credit Card: Using a credit card incurs an additional 4.5% transaction fee, so we recommend other methods if possible.
Enjoy hassle-free shopping in Korea with Coupang’s fast and flexible Rocket Delivery! If you have any questions or need help, just let us know. Happy shopping!

source.sourceTextFile: wa_msgs/WhatsApp Chat with COZE L9 13thApr Phang Jongshing 5A2K1B/WhatsApp Chat with COZE L9 13thApr Phang Jongshing 5A2K1B.txt

examples.question: @⁨COZE_Online⁩ I have one question. Where do we put food waste like banana peel and similar? In general waste or do you have food waste plastic bags? Seems to much to put in drain.
examples.answer:
🗑️ Waste Disposal Guide in Seoul
(For Foreign Residents in Our Accommodation)
Welcome to Seoul! 🌆 Korea has a very organized (and strict!) waste disposal system. We’ve made it super simple for you during your stay — just follow the steps below, and let us take care of the rest. ♻️😊
1️⃣ General Waste (일반 쓰레기 – Non-Recyclables)
🧻 What goes in:
Small plastic wrappers, dirty packaging
Used tissues, cotton swabs, sanitary items
Chicken bones, large fish bones 🐔🐟
Rubber gloves, sponges, toothbrushes
🚫 Please DON’T take your trash outside yourself — Seoul’s system can be tricky. ✅ DO THIS INSTEAD:
Use the indoor trash bins for non-recyclables
Separate this from recyclables
🛎️ Let our staff know when it’s full — we’ll collect it for you!
2️⃣ Food Waste (음식물 쓰레기 – Organic Waste)
🥕 What goes in:
Leftover rice, noodles, bread, fruits & veggies
Eggshells, coffee grounds ☕
Small fish bones
🧯 How to dispose:
Use the sink garbage disposer in the kitchen
Turn on the water 💧, drop in soft food scraps, and grind ⚠️ DON’T put in hard or fibrous waste (like bones, shells, onion peels or corn husks) — they can break the system!
Chicken bones & large scraps → go in general waste
3️⃣ Recyclables (재활용 – Clean & Sorted Only!)
♻️ What can be recycled? Plastics 🧴
Bottles, takeout containers — rinse before disposal Glass 🍾
Wine bottles, jars — no food inside! Paper & Cardboard 📦
Newspapers, flattened boxes, clean cartons Cans & Metals 🥫

examples.answer:
🍽️ *How to Order Food with the Guest Smartphone* (Coupang Eats)
Welcome to COZE Hospitality 3.0! Here’s the easy way to get your favorite dishes delivered — even if your own credit card won’t work in Korea. 👇
---
1️⃣ Grab the fully-set guest phone 📱
Open Coupang Eats (already logged in and location-ready).
2️⃣ Fill your cart with deliciousness 🍕🍜🥟
Browse local restaurants, add everything you’d like, mix and match freely!
3️⃣ Take a full scroll screenshot 🖼️
Before tapping “Pay,” swipe through the entire cart and capture it (most phones: Power + Vol-Down → “Scroll Capture”).
4️⃣ Send the screenshot to the Concierge chat 💬
Drop the image into your dedicated channel — we’ll check and confirm right away.
5️⃣ We pay for you 💳✨
Our team places the order using a Korean card so your food heads straight to your door.
6️⃣ Settle up at checkout 💵
Simply reimburse us in cash when you check out.
Card or bank transfer is possible, but please note they trigger extra fees (double VAT on transfers and foreign-card surcharges). Cash is easiest and cheapest!
Why this system?
Korean apps require local authentication, so overseas cards are blocked. The guest phone lets you shop, but cannot charge your own card. Think of our card as a friendly loan — the exact food cost is passed back to you, not treated as COZE revenue.
Enjoy your meal and your stay! 🍽️🏠😊

examples.question:
Hello Gaya.
I was wondering, does your company have another property that is located more in downtown, e.g. City Hall, Myungdong, Insadong, DDM area?
We have 5 young adults and teenagers who wants to have transportation convenience where they are able to comeback & rest at the stay through out the day.  They plan to stay out late & commute by trains.
The villa we booked is a bit far away from the city center.  By car it won't take too long to return to villa but it might not serve the children's needs.
In case there are other properties that you can recommend, we need 4-5 rooms, 5-6 beds, and around the same price & same traveling dates.  We will have 9 adults total (1 more than what we booked for)
examples.answer:
Hello @⁨BS_Sophia HWANG⁩ 😊
Thank you so much for sharing your thoughts with us.
We completely understand your concern, especially since the young adults and teenagers may want to move around Seoul freely, come back to rest during the day, and stay out late using the subway.
To be very honest, areas like City Hall, Myeongdong, Insadong, and DDM are very convenient, but because of the nature of those downtown neighborhoods, the buildings are usually smaller, the streets can be quite noisy at night, and the air quality is often not ideal due to traffic, restaurants, and cigarette smoke. Even if the number of rooms looks suitable, it is quite difficult for us to operate a truly comfortable, high-quality COZE-style stay in those areas.

examples.answer:
🌟 Expense & Payment 🌟
During your stay, you might incur various expenses such as airport van pick-ups 🚐, food deliveries 🍕, shopping orders 📦, or exciting tours 🎡! Our COZE team usually covers these expenses upfront or provides these services directly to make your stay more convenient and comfortable. 😊
💳 Here's how our payment settlement system works:
📌 Settlement Timing:
We'll summarize your expenses and inform you about the total cost one day before your check-out. You can conveniently settle all charges at once. You can pay directly to our team when you check out :)
💡 Payment Methods & Tips:
1️⃣ Cash (Recommended) 💵 Cash payment is the most economical option as it avoids the non-refundable 10% VAT charge.
2️⃣ Cryptocurrency (e.g., USDT) 🪙 You can easily pay with cryptocurrency, which is becoming increasingly popular among our guests!
3️⃣ WISE APP https://wise.com/us/money-transfer-app/
*WISE uses the real exchange rate with transparent fees, helping you save money. very cheap!!*
However, please note this will incur an additional 10% VAT.
4️⃣ Local Bank Transfer 🏦
You can also transfer the amount using your currency from your local bank to our bank in your country(*It's like local bank transfer) However  these below currency are available. Also 10% VAT should be added
USD / KRW / EUR / AUD / CNY / GBP / HKD / JPY / SGD
5️⃣  Credit Card 💳 (Least Recommended)
Paying by credit card involves extra fees:
Approximately 4.5% credit card service fee.
Payment is charged in Korean Won (KRW), leading to double currency conversion rates.
Potential overseas transaction fees imposed by your card issuer.
Due to these factors, credit card payments can become significantly more expensive. 😥
We highly recommend using cash or cryptocurrency for convenience and cost-effectiveness. ✨
Thank you for your understanding and cooperation! Enjoy your lovely stay with COZE. 💖
Warm regards,
Gaya, Guest Care Team of COZE Hospitality 3.0

examples.question:
Hello COZE: Following up on the Karaoke theme I have done a little research and it seems private rooms for 7 people not very common. What are your thoughts on this one??? 나이스 노래방 22 Bogwang-ro 60-gil, Itaewon-dong, Yongsan District, Seoul, South Korea
02-790-6268. I can't find a website for it so I don't know the cost or details, but could you query if they have a private room for 7 adults, English song system, 7–9pm on Tuesday 19th May. and the cost please? Is it normal to pay in advance for these?
examples.answer:
Hello 😊 Thank you so much for checking and sharing this option with us.
In Korea, most karaoke places are actually “noraebang,” which means private-room karaoke. So for 7 adults, it is usually not a problem at all — many places have rooms that can fit 7 people comfortably, so please don’t worry too much about the room size.
The only point to keep in mind is the English song selection. Most Korean karaoke systems do have English songs, but the collection can be somewhat limited, and the newest English-based songs may not always be updated quickly. Classic and popular English songs are usually much easier to find.
For the place you found, Nice Noraebang in Itaewon, they may not have a website, which is very common for local karaoke places in Korea. We can help check directly by phone whether they have:
• A private room for 7 adults
• English song system
• Availability from 7–9 PM on Tuesday, May 19
• The estimated cost
If you would like the most polished and “luxury” karaoke experience, we would personally recommend Su Noraebang Luxury Hongdae Branch. Hongdae is one of the most popular nightlife areas in Seoul, so the karaoke places there are generally more stylish, lively, and guest-friendly.
Su Noraebang Luxury Hongdae Branch
https://naver.me/FzSZEtUp
If you prefer something closer and more convenient near the accommodation, Philharmony Norae Practice Room is also a good nearby option.
Philharmony Norae Practice Room
https://naver.me/xExkk7RG
For payment, it is usually not normal to pay fully in advance for a regular noraebang. Most places accept walk-ins or phone reservations, and guests usually pay on-site. Sometimes they may ask for a name and phone number for reservation, but advance payment is not usually required unless it is a very special venue or a very busy time.
We’ll be happy to help check the availability and cost, then guide you to the best option for a fun karaoke night in Seoul 🎤✨
Gaya, Guest Care Team of COZE Hospitality 3.0

**Climate Card (기후동행카드)**에 대한 영문 사용 설명서(User Guide)입니다. 외국인 게스트나 친구에게 바로 공유하실 수 있도록 깔끔하게 정리했습니다.
# 📋 User Guide: Seoul Climate Card (기후동행카드)
The **Climate Card** is a prepaid transit card that offers unlimited access to Seoul’s public transportation (subway, buses, and the Tashu/Ttareungi public bicycle system) for a fixed price during a specific period.
## 1. Where to Buy & Top Up
### 💳 Physical Card (Recommended for Foreigners)
 * **Where to Buy:** You can purchase the physical card for **3,000 KRW (Cash only)** at:
   * Customer Service Centers inside Seoul Subway Stations (Line 1–8).
   * Convenience stores near subway stations (GS25, CU, 7-Eleven, Emart24).
 * **Where to Top Up:** At the automated ticket vending machines located inside Seoul subway stations **(Cash only)**.
> ⚠️ **Note:** Mobile cards are currently only available for Android users with a domestic resident registration number/Korean credit card. Foreign tourists are highly recommended to use the **Physical Card**.
>
## 2. Pass Types & Fares
You can select the duration that best fits your travel itinerary:
| Pass Type | Price (Without Bicycle) | Price (With Ttareungi Bicycle) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1-Day Pass** | 5,000 KRW | 5,500 KRW |
| **2-Day Pass** | 8,000 KRW | 8,500 KRW |
| **3-Day Pass** | 10,000 KRW | 10,500 KRW |
| **5-Day Pass** | 15,000 KRW | 15,500 KRW |
| **30-Day Pass** | 62,000 KRW | 65,000 KRW |
*The pass becomes valid immediately upon topping up at the machine.*
## 3. Coverage & Boundaries
### ✅ What is INCLUDED:
 * **Seoul Subway Lines:** All lines within the Seoul city boundary (Lines 1–9, Ui-Sinseol Line, Sillim Line, Gyeongui-Jungang Line, Gyeongchun Line, Suin-Bundang Line, and Airport Railroad **local trains**).
 * **Seoul City Buses:** Blue, Green, Maeul (local community), and Night (Late-night) buses.
### ❌ What is EXCLUDED:
 * **Shinbundang Line** (due to a different fare system).
 * **Express Buses, Intercity Buses, and Red Buses** (Gyeonggi-do/Incheon licensed buses entering Seoul).
 * **Airport Railroad Express Trains (AREX).**
 * *Getting off outside Seoul:* If you board within Seoul but get off at a station outside the designated network (e.g., deep into Gyeonggi-do), you must pay the extra fare to the station staff.
## 4. How to Use
### 🚇 On Subways & Buses
 1. **Tap ON:** Tap your card against the sensor on the turnstile or bus card reader when boarding.
 2. **Tap OFF (CRITICAL):** You **MUST tap your card again when exiting** the subway or bus.
   * *If you fail to tap off twice within a short period, your card will be suspended/locked for 24 hours.*
### 🚲 On Ttareungi (Seoul Public Bicycles)
*If you purchased a pass that includes bicycle access:*
 1. Download the **'Ttareungi (Seoul Bike)'** app.
 2. Register your Climate Card number in the app to issue a voucher.
 3. Use the app to scan the QR code on the bike to unlock it.
## 💡 Quick Tips for Visitors
 * **One Card Per Person:** Each traveler must have their own physical Climate Card; it cannot be shared or tapped multiple times for multiple people.
 * **Keep Cash Handy:** Both purchasing the physical card and topping it up at the station machines require **Korean Won cash**. Credit cards are not accepted at the vending machines for tourist passes.

examples.question:
Thank you! Will ask any questions on here :)
May I ask if I can come a few hours earlier on Nov 8th to set up before everyone else gets to the Airbnb?
Please let me know what time is best 🙏🏼 thank you so much!!!
examples.answer:
Hello, @⁨~Ami⁩ 💛
Thank you so much for reaching out — we’re truly excited to welcome you to our home!
Since our retreat is reserved for just one group at a time (not like big hotels with endless rooms), early check-in and late check-out are sometimes limited. But don’t worry — we’ve prepared some flexible and guest-loved solutions so your trip can still feel smooth and stress-free ✨
---
🌟 Early Check-in & Late Check-out
If the calendar is open, we’d be delighted to offer them for free!
But because other guests often check out right before your stay (and in right after), we may not always confirm this in advance.
That said — here are some great alternatives that past guests found super helpful:
---
Option 1. 🧳 Luggage Drop at Home
Arrive early or fly late? Simply leave your bags at the house between 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM.
You’ll be free to explore without heavy luggage and return when check-in or airport time comes.
It’s simple, practical, and lets you start (or end) your day light and happy.
---
Option 2. ✨ Driver-Assisted Luggage Service
A. Arriving very early (before 10:00 AM)
Your driver collects your luggage straight from the airport and brings it to our office.
Go enjoy Seoul right away, and we’ll place your bags inside your home before 3:00 PM.
Fee: ₩30,000 per driver (South of Hanriver 20K KRW extra)
B. Leaving late (after 3:00 PM)
Your driver picks up luggage at the house and keeps it safe until your airport departure.
You can roam freely, and he’ll meet you anywhere in the Gangbuk area before heading to the airport.
Fee: ₩30,000 per driver (South of Hanriver 20K KRW extra)
Perfect for those who want convenience and comfort without luggage worries.
---
Option 3. 🧭 Private Tour with Tour Host(Guide & Storyteller)
Turn arrival or departure day into a sightseeing day!
Your dedicated tour host will guide you through hidden gems and local favorites while handling all the transfers.
This way, your “waiting hours” transform into one of the best memories of your trip.
Guests often call this the smartest way to make the most of their time.
---
Option 4. 🛏️ Book an Extra Night
If you’re traveling with children, elderly family, or simply value total peace of mind, adding one more night is the ultimate comfort.
No rush, no stress — just rest, unpack, and enjoy your cozy space at your own pace.
Sometimes, that extra night is the secret to a smoother and happier journey.
---
💫 Whichever option you choose, we’ll make sure your stay feels easy, joyful, and tailored just for you. If you’d like us to arrange any of these in advance, simply let us know — we’re always here with a happy heart.
With warmth & good vibes,
Gaya | Guest Care Team of COZE Hospitality 3.0

**Climate Card (기후동행카드)**에 대한 영문 사용 설명서(User Guide)입니다. 외국인 게스트나 친구에게 바로 공유하실 수 있도록 깔끔하게 정리했습니다.
# 📋 User Guide: Seoul Climate Card (기후동행카드)
The **Climate Card** is a prepaid transit card that offers unlimited access to Seoul’s public transportation (subway, buses, and the Tashu/Ttareungi public bicycle system) for a fixed price during a specific period.
## 1. Where to Buy & Top Up
### 💳 Physical Card (Recommended for Foreigners)
 * **Where to Buy:** You can purchase the physical card for **3,000 KRW (Cash only)** at:
   * Customer Service Centers inside Seoul Subway Stations (Line 1–8).
   * Convenience stores near subway stations (GS25, CU, 7-Eleven, Emart24).
 * **Where to Top Up:** At the automated ticket vending machines located inside Seoul subway stations **(Cash only)**.
> ⚠️ **Note:** Mobile cards are currently only available for Android users with a domestic resident registration number/Korean credit card. Foreign tourists are highly recommended to use the **Physical Card**.
>
## 2. Pass Types & Fares
You can select the duration that best fits your travel itinerary:
| Pass Type | Price (Without Bicycle) | Price (With Ttareungi Bicycle) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1-Day Pass** | 5,000 KRW | 5,500 KRW |
| **2-Day Pass** | 8,000 KRW | 8,500 KRW |
| **3-Day Pass** | 10,000 KRW | 10,500 KRW |
| **5-Day Pass** | 15,000 KRW | 15,500 KRW |
| **30-Day Pass** | 62,000 KRW | 65,000 KRW |
*The pass becomes valid immediately upon topping up at the machine.*
## 3. Coverage & Boundaries
### ✅ What is INCLUDED:
 * **Seoul Subway Lines:** All lines within the Seoul city boundary (Lines 1–9, Ui-Sinseol Line, Sillim Line, Gyeongui-Jungang Line, Gyeongchun Line, Suin-Bundang Line, and Airport Railroad **local trains**).
 * **Seoul City Buses:** Blue, Green, Maeul (local community), and Night (Late-night) buses.
### ❌ What is EXCLUDED:
 * **Shinbundang Line** (due to a different fare system).
 * **Express Buses, Intercity Buses, and Red Buses** (Gyeonggi-do/Incheon licensed buses entering Seoul).
 * **Airport Railroad Express Trains (AREX).**
 * *Getting off outside Seoul:* If you board within Seoul but get off at a station outside the designated network (e.g., deep into Gyeonggi-do), you must pay the extra fare to the station staff.
## 4. How to Use
### 🚇 On Subways & Buses
 1. **Tap ON:** Tap your card against the sensor on the turnstile or bus card reader when boarding.
 2. **Tap OFF (CRITICAL):** You **MUST tap your card again when exiting** the subway or bus.
   * *If you fail to tap off twice within a short period, your card will be suspended/locked for 24 hours.*
### 🚲 On Ttareungi (Seoul Public Bicycles)
*If you purchased a pass that includes bicycle access:*
 1. Download the **'Ttareungi (Seoul Bike)'** app.
 2. Register your Climate Card number in the app to issue a voucher.
 3. Use the app to scan the QR code on the bike to unlock it.
## 💡 Quick Tips for Visitors
 * **One Card Per Person:** Each traveler must have their own physical Climate Card; it cannot be shared or tapped multiple times for multiple people.
 * **Keep Cash Handy:** Both purchasing the physical card and topping it up at the station machines require **Korean Won cash**. Credit cards are not accepted at the vending machines for tourist passes.

examples.question:
Thanks. Maybe as part of this procedure one cock roach showed up and we found it in room 3 bed.
What is your recommendation if more show up and for how to get our stuff and luggage bug safe?
examples.answer:
Hello, Dear Guest 💛
Thank you so much for reaching out — we’re truly excited to welcome you to our home!
Since our retreat is reserved for just one group at a time (not like big hotels with endless rooms), early check-in and late check-out are sometimes limited. But don’t worry — we’ve prepared some flexible and guest-loved solutions so your trip can still feel smooth and stress-free ✨
---
🌟 Early Check-in & Late Check-out
If the calendar is open, we’d be delighted to offer them for free!
But because other guests often check out right before your stay (and in right after), we may not always confirm this in advance.
That said — here are some great alternatives that past guests found super helpful:
---
Option 1. 🧳 Luggage Drop at Home
Arrive early or fly late? Simply leave your bags at the house between 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM.
You’ll be free to explore without heavy luggage and return when check-in or airport time comes.
It’s simple, practical, and lets you start (or end) your day light and happy.
---
Option 2. ✨ Driver-Assisted Luggage Service
A. Arriving very early (before 10:00 AM)
Your driver collects your luggage straight from the airport and brings it to our office.
Go enjoy Seoul right away, and we’ll place your bags inside your home before 3:00 PM.
Fee: ₩30,000 per driver (South of Hanriver 20K KRW extra)
B. Leaving late (after 3:00 PM)
Your driver picks up luggage at the house and keeps it safe until your airport departure.
You can roam freely, and he’ll meet you anywhere in the Gangbuk area before heading to the airport.
Fee: ₩30,000 per driver (South of Hanriver 20K KRW extra)
Perfect for those who want convenience and comfort without luggage worries.
---
Option 3. 🧭 Private Tour with Tour Host(Guide & Storyteller)
Turn arrival or departure day into a sightseeing day!
Your dedicated tour host will guide you through hidden gems and local favorites while handling all the transfers.
This way, your “waiting hours” transform into one of the best memories of your trip.
Guests often call this the smartest way to make the most of their time.
---
Option 4. 🛏️ Book an Extra Night
If you’re traveling with children, elderly family, or simply value total peace of mind, adding one more night is the ultimate comfort.
No rush, no stress — just rest, unpack, and enjoy your cozy space at your own pace.
Sometimes, that extra night is the secret to a smoother and happier journey.
---
💫 Whichever option you choose, we’ll make sure your stay feels easy, joyful, and tailored just for you. If you’d like us to arrange any of these in advance, simply let us know — we’re always here with a happy heart.
With warmth & good vibes,
Gaya | Guest Care Team of COZE Hospitality 3.0
